summary: Russia announced the liberation of the settlement Priut in the DNR and reported shooting down 185 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and the Azov Sea. Ukraine reported multiple Shahed drone attacks across several oblasts and a possible medium‑range ballistic missile launch from the Kapustin Yar test site. New Russian drone launch sites near the Belarusian border were identified via satellite imagery.
key_points: Russia said its forces liberated the settlement Priut in the DNR (Source: @tass_agency/380442).
The Russian Ministry of Defense reported shooting down 185 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Azov Sea (Source: @tass_agency/380436).
Ukraine reported a large Shahed drone attack involving 117 drones, with 102 shot down, affecting multiple locations including Orël, Kursk and Crimea (Source: @kpszsu/64903).
A possible medium‑range ballistic missile launch was warned from the Kapustin Yar test site (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
Satellite analysis identified at least five new Russian drone launch complexes near the Belarusian border in Bryansk, Oryol and Smolensk regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
Ukrainian forces struck a Nova Poshta mail terminal in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30864).
Reports of a ballistic missile launch from Crimea were posted (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67580).

sections.executive_summary: Russia announced the liberation of Priut in the DNR and claimed to have downed 185 Ukrainian UAVs over its territory and the Azov Sea. Ukraine reported a massive Shahed drone swarm attack, with 102 drones intercepted, and warned of a possible medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar. Satellite imagery revealed new Russian drone launch sites near the Belarusian border, expanding strike capabilities.

points: Ukrainian forces struck a Nova Poshta mail service terminal in Zaporizhzhia, indicating targeting of logistics infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30864).

Multiple Shahed drone attacks were reported across several oblasts, including a coordinated strike of 117 drones with 102 shot down, affecting locations such as Orël, Kursk, and Crimea (Source: @kpszsu/64903).
Ukrainian air defenses engaged and downed numerous hostile UAVs, including guided and strike drones over Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, and Kharkiv regions (Source: @eRadarrua/96345, @eRadarrua/96312).
Ukrainian officials warned of a possible medium‑range ballistic missile launch from the Kapustin Yar test site, highlighting heightened missile threat (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
The Russian Ministry of Defense reported shooting down 185 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Azov Sea (Source: @tass_agency/380436).
Russia announced the liberation of the settlement Priut in the DNR, claiming territorial gain (Source: @tass_agency/380442).
Five new drone launch complexes were identified near the Belarusian border in Bryansk, Oryol and Smolensk regions, with the largest near the village of Tsymbulova (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
TASS reported no casualties after an explosion at the former DNR security minister's house in Novaya Moskva (Source: @tass_agency/380442).
Shahed drone swarms attacked from directions including Orël, Kursk, Millerovo, Chauda and Gvardeyskoye, targeting eastern Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/64903).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAV activity over the Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts, with strike drones heading toward Poltava and Kharkiv (Source: @eRadarrua/96345, @eRadarrua/96312).
A Nova Poshta terminal in Zaporizhzhia was struck, suggesting Russian targeting of logistics in the east (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30864).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV sightings along the Dnipro River corridor near Pavlograd (Source: @kpszsu/64937).
Ukrainian radar reported UAVs along the Dnipro River near Dobryanka in Chernihiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/96348).
Ukrainian sources noted UAV activity on the border of Kharkiv and Sumy regions, heading southwest (Source: @kpszsu/64901).
Reports of UAVs near the northern approaches of Kyiv were logged, with an air alarm at 22:30 on 12 June (Source: @eRadarrua/96344).
A threat of hostile tactical aviation was announced for the north‑east direction, indicating possible air raids (Source: @kpszsu/64912).
Ukrainian forces reported a strike on a Nova Poshta terminal in Zaporizhzhia, a key southern logistics hub (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30864).
UAVs were observed over the Black Sea heading toward the Zatoka area (Source: @eRadarrua/96316).
Russian claims of downing Ukrainian UAVs over the Azov Sea suggest active aerial contest in the southern maritime zone (Source: @tass_agency/380436).
A possible ballistic missile launch from Crimea was reported, indicating heightened threat to southern regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67580).
Russia reported shooting down 185 Ukrainian UAVs over its territory and the Azov Sea (Source: @tass_agency/380436).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 102 Shahed and other drones during a large attack, with 14 drones hitting seven locations (Source: @kpszsu/64903).
Ukrainian radar logged multiple UAV sightings across central and northern Ukraine, prompting several air alerts (Source: @eRadarrua/96341, @eRadarrua/96344).
Threats of medium‑range ballistic missile use from Kapustin Yar were announced, raising concerns of new air‑to‑ground capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/64925).

key_developments: Russia claims to have liberated Priut in the DNR, indicating a localized territorial gain (Source: @tass_agency/380442).
The Russian Ministry of Defense reports downing 185 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and the Azov Sea (Source: @tass_agency/380436).
Ukraine reports a massive Shahed drone swarm attack with 117 drones, 102 intercepted, affecting multiple front‑line locations (Source: @kpszsu/64903).
Satellite imagery reveals five new Russian drone launch sites near the Belarusian border, expanding strike depth (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
A possible medium‑range ballistic missile launch was warned from the Kapustin Yar test range (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
Ukrainian forces struck a Nova Poshta terminal in Zaporizhzhia, targeting logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30864).
Reports of a ballistic missile launch from Crimea suggest escalation of missile threats in the south (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67580).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are extending their strike capabilities with new drone launch complexes near Belarus, enabling deeper penetration into Ukrainian airspace. The reported medium‑range ballistic missile threat from Kapustin Yar adds a new dimension to the southern front, potentially targeting critical infrastructure. Ukrainian air defenses continue to intercept large UAV swarms, but the high volume of attacks indicates sustained Russian aerial pressure across multiple fronts.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is intensifying air defense alerts and mobilizing anti‑drone assets across the north, east, and south, as evidenced by numerous radar reports and air alarms. International partners are monitoring the emergence of new Russian drone bases, which may prompt adjustments in NATO air patrols near the Belarusian border. The reported liberation of Priut may be used by Russia to claim progress, influencing diplomatic narratives.
sections.intelligence_notes: Satellite verification of new drone launch sites near the Belarusian border should be prioritized for targeting. Monitoring of Kapustin Yar activity is essential to anticipate potential missile launches. Continued collection of UAV flight paths will aid in refining Ukrainian air defense deployment and predicting future attack vectors.
